title: Environmental Impact Assessment
the_principle: A product sold on environmental stewardship must account for its OWN footprint, honestly, including the harms. An environmental impact section that lists only benefits is not an assessment; it is an advertisement.

phase_framing.statement: AquaVision is now three layers, and the ENVIRONMENTAL footprint and impact story below belongs almost entirely to Phase 3 (the OmniDrone/IoT/PondTwin hardware layer) — it is unchanged from the original hardware-first assessment and is reproduced as-is under that label.

phase_1_2_environmental_footprint.statement: The Phase 1/2 software layer (free app, premium analytics, marketplace) has a materially different and much smaller direct footprint: mobile/web compute and data storage, not batteries, airframes, or sondes deployed on the water. Its environmental relevance is indirect — better feeding decisions and disease-prevention guidance logged and surfaced to farmers who previously kept no records at all could reduce feed waste and antimicrobial overuse at the behavioural level, before any sensor is involved.
phase_1_2_environmental_footprint.the_honest_caveat: This is a hypothesis about behaviour change, not a measured effect, and it is weaker and slower than the sensor-driven Phase 3 mechanism below because it depends entirely on farmers acting on free-tier guidance with no verification loop. It also inherits the same over-stocking-style tension: better production guidance could as easily support intensification as restraint. No lifecycle assessment of the compute/hosting footprint has been done and is not a near-term priority relative to the Phase 3 hardware assessment below.
phase_1_2_environmental_footprint.status: not measured, not modeled — noted for completeness only

phase_3_hardware_environmental_impact.note: Everything below this point is the ORIGINAL hardware-layer environmental assessment, unchanged in substance, now explicitly scoped to Phase 3 (OmniDrone, docks, IoT sondes, PondTwin) rather than to the company as a whole.

positive_impacts.impact: Reduced eutrophication of shared water bodies
positive_impacts.mechanism: Condition-aware feeding reduces uneaten feed, the primary driver of the nutrient loading that degrades the water. Carrying-capacity analytics support collective management.
positive_impacts.magnitude: Potentially significant, UNPROVEN. Depends on farms actually feeding on evidence and on carrying-capacity analytics being adopted.
positive_impacts.the_caveat: Undercut by the over-stocking paradox — the same instruments could enable intensification that increases loading. Net effect is genuinely uncertain and must not be claimed as unambiguously positive.

positive_impacts.mechanism: Precision placement delivers a given effect with less total substance.
positive_impacts.the_caveat: Cuts both ways — easier dosing can increase total use. See ethics-data-privacy.json#/the_antimicrobial_stewardship_tension.

positive_impacts.mechanism: Sheen, effluent, and debris detection supports faster response to pollution events in shared waters.
Continuous multi-farm environmental data is exactly what an ecosystem approach to aquaculture (EAA) requires and currently lacks.
positive_impacts.magnitude: Potentially the largest positive impact — but it requires the cross-farm data governance problem to be solved, which is unresolved.

the_products_own_footprint.note: The harms the solution itself causes. Named honestly.

impacts.detail: Lithium packs are a consumable, replaced frequently given the sortie cadence and dock heat. Drones, docks, and sondes are electronic hardware with a disposal footprint. A distributed network multiplies this.
impacts.mitigation: Battery recycling programme; design for repairability and component reuse; honest lifecycle accounting rather than ignoring it.

impacts.detail: A drone that ditches puts lithium and electronics into a food-production water body. This is why containment and buoyancy are first-class requirements — but the residual risk is non-zero.
impacts.mitigation: Positive buoyancy, lithium containment, locator beacon, water-aware failsafe. See airframe-propulsion.json#/the_ditching_provision.
impacts.the_honest_note: A sunk drone in a lake people fish from is an environmental incident, not just a lost asset. We design against it and we disclose the residual risk.

impacts.detail: Carbon composite, rare-earth motor magnets, and sensor electronics carry embodied impacts.
impacts.mitigation: Beyond our early control, but worth honest accounting rather than omission.

impacts.detail: Modest per sortie, and solar-powered docks make it largely renewable in operation. This is the one footprint line that is genuinely small.
A drone over a lake disturbs birds and other wildlife. Repeated daily overflight has a non-zero ecological cost, especially near breeding or roosting sites.
impacts.mitigation: Flight-path planning around sensitive areas; NOT using the platform for active wildlife deterrence (a deliberate exclusion — see problem-taxonomy.json bio-predation).

the_net_assessment.statement: The net environmental impact is genuinely uncertain and depends on adoption patterns we cannot yet predict.
the_net_assessment.the_honest_reasoning: If the product reduces feed waste and supports carrying-capacity management, the benefit likely exceeds the hardware footprint. If it primarily enables intensification and easier chemical dosing, the net could be negative. The outcome depends on HOW it is adopted and marketed, not just on what it does — which places a real responsibility on the company's own choices.
the_net_assessment.why_state_it_this_way: Because claiming a confident net-positive impact at this stage would be exactly the sustainability-washing the field is full of. The intellectually honest position is that the sign of the net impact is a function of the company's own discipline, and that is both true and a stronger statement than a fabricated positive number.

lifecycle_thinking.commitment: Before scale, conduct a real lifecycle assessment of the hardware, not a hand-wave. A distributed network of hundreds of drones, docks, and sondes has a material footprint that a single-unit intuition misses.
lifecycle_thinking.status: not done — flagged as a pre-scale requirement
